specimen_label: abc/Szpiro monotone decrease at fixed bad-prime count (Ergon 2026-04-16)
tautology_warning: Pattern 1 lineage check: szpiro_ratio = log|disc| / log(N). Both numerator and denominator encode the same primes (bad primes dominate log|disc|, and conductor is the product of bad-prime powers). Stratifying by num_bad_primes is the NATURAL tautology control: at fixed k, the discriminant's bad-prime structure is held roughly constant, leaving the residual szpiro-vs-logN relationship to reflect (a) conductor spread within the bad-prime set and (b) the specific primes chosen. Effect survives P021 by construction if it is not purely confound-driven; this is what Ergon observed.

P051_unfolding.reason: P051 N(T) unfolding is a zero-spacing concept. No natural analog for scalar szpiro_ratio. Flagging as 0 (untested) in invariance profile rather than forcing a mismatched test.

P052_decontamination.interpretation: Residualizing szpiro on k is a constant shift within each k, so within-k slopes are identical to the raw P021 slopes. The interesting number is the POOLED residual slope: if it remains strongly negative, there is residual conductor-dependence after removing the stratum effect. If it collapses to ~0, the pooled monotone signal was entirely k-mediated (and the within-k residual slope is the real effect).

monotone_check.note: "Monotone decreasing in k" means the within-k slope gets MORE negative as bad-prime count grows — i.e., the szpiro-vs-logN trend is steepest for many-bad-prime curves.

invariance_profile.P021_num_bad_primes: +2 (z=-6.9 to -22.7 per stratum under permutation null; within-k slope is strongly object-level)
invariance_profile.P020_conductor_binned: +1 (szpiro vs k within conductor bin is positive and coherent: slope +0.44 to +0.61 at adequate bins; the two stratified views together are self-consistent)
invariance_profile.P042_within_stratum_perm: +2 (real slope significantly exceeds null in every adequate stratum)
invariance_profile.P051_unfolding: 0 (NOT_APPLICABLE: zero-spacing concept has no direct scalar-szpiro analog)
invariance_profile.P052_decontamination: -1 (pooled slope -0.597 collapses to -0.069 after residualizing szpiro on k — 88% of pooled signal is num_bad_primes-mediated; only 12% residual)

ergon_claim_reproduction.refinement: The direction is correct — higher bad-prime curves have steeper negative szpiro-vs-logN slopes on average (k=1 weakest at -0.128; k=3-6 cluster around -0.35 to -0.49). The strict monotone statement is inaccurate and should be restated as: slope is negative for all k and *generally more negative* for k>=2 than k=1, with a non-monotone plateau at k>=2.

pattern_1_tautology_quantified.finding: 88% of the pooled szpiro-vs-logN slope is k-mediated (pooled -0.597 -> residual -0.069 after removing k-mean).
pattern_1_tautology_quantified.interpretation: The pooled -0.597 is mostly the marginal-distribution confound between conductor and num_bad_primes, not structural. The real structure is the decomposition: within-k slope (negative, strong) and within-logN slope (positive vs k, strong) — these two projections are the non-tautological content of F015.
pattern_1_tautology_quantified.tautology_status: PARTIAL_TAUTOLOGY. The within-stratum effects are NOT tautological (they survive permutation nulls at z<-6 consistently). But any report that leads with the pooled -0.597 number is reporting 88% confound.

connection_to_prior_work.F011_F013_parallel: F015 joins F011 and F013 in showing that the right coordinate system is the STRATIFICATION, not the pooled view. F011 was uniform across 7 projections = axis class exhausted. F013 was density-mediated = right axis is unfolding. F015 is k-confounded = right axes are (k, logN) joint. Common shape: single-axis pooled views conceal the real structure; stratification or unfolding reveals it.
connection_to_prior_work.pattern_17_case: Language matters: the correct phrasing is "szpiro-vs-logN slope is negative within every bad-prime stratum, with magnitude generally increasing for k>=2" not "monotone decrease." The old phrasing smuggles in a stronger claim than the data supports.

recommended_followups: Re-test with k=7..12 strata (need to check ec_curvedata coverage). If slopes cluster at -0.35 to -0.49 regardless of k>=2, the effect saturates in k.

Apply P020 x P021 joint stratification: within each (conductor-bin, k-bin) cell, does the conductor-within-k slope persist? Current data has n>=100 only in {log10N in [4,5] or >=5} x {k=1..6}; joint is feasible.
Investigate the k=1 outlier separately: slope -0.128 (vs -0.35+ for k>=2). Either k=1 sample is biased small (only 5000 total, low-N dominated) or k=1 curves really are structurally different.

Update F015 tensor description: "monotone" claim is incorrect literally; re-describe as "szpiro-vs-logN slope is negative within every bad-prime stratum with magnitude saturating for k>=2; 88% of pooled slope is k-mediated confound."
